headlines.title: 6,600 noncitizens were registered to vote in N.J. because of a ‘software error,’ governor says
headlines.description: Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a review found that around 400 noncitizens cast ballots in elections from 2023 to 2024. The DOJ said it’s investigating the matter.

headlines.title: Secretary Hegseth grilled over growing cost of war with Iran
headlines.description: Iran launched new attacks on U.S. allies in the region today even as Secretary Hegseth made his case to a Senate committee for more funding. The hearing turned contentious with a verbal dispute over leadership. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

headlines.title: Hegseth says Iran war will cost $37.5 billion
headlines.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told senators that he estimates the Iran war has cost the U.S. military $37.5 billion so far. That estimate is an increase from the $28 billion quoted in the spring, before a ceasefire went into effect. NBC News reported this month that some experts and officials have put the overall cost for the war at $80 billion to $100 billion.

headlines.title: ‘Voters want change’: Michigan Senate Candidate Abdul El-Sayed on tight primary race
headlines.description: Michigan Democratic Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss his bid against Rep. Haley Stevens in one of the most closely-watched Democratic races that’s become a referendum on the direction of the Democratic Party. El-Sayed discusses U.S. military aid to Israel and his calls to abolish ICE.

headlines.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
headlines.description: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: U.S. attorney who served for 54 minutes sues over firing by Trump
headlines.description: The Trump administration has fired several U.S. attorneys appointed by federal judges. Roger Rogoff is the first to sue over being removed.

headlines.title: What to watch for in Arizona tonight: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Ben Kamisar previews the key contests to keep an eye on as primary results roll in across Arizona. Plus, our White House team digs into a critical new phase of the Iran war for President Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Appeals court rejects Biden’s bid to block release of recordings of ghostwriter interviews
headlines.description: The panel’s 2-1 ruling late Monday found there is a “substantial” public interest in disclosing the material that Joe Biden wants to keep under wraps.

headlines.title: Sen. Darline Graham says she’s running for a full 6-year term after Trump urged her to jump into the race
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said he had given his “complete and total endorsement” to Graham, who was appointed to finish her late brother’s Senate term.

headlines.title: Democrats avoid a messy fight in Maine, but uncertainty lingers: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail.

headlines.title: Trump says new Air Force One will be sent away for upgrades amid security concerns
headlines.description: President Trump said the Air Force One plane that was donated by Qatar will be sent away for upgrades amid swirling concerns about its security features.

headlines.title: Top Maine Senate candidates end campaigns as Troy Jackson builds Democratic support
headlines.description: Former state Senate President Troy Jackson is racking up support to become Maine’s next Democratic Senate nominee, with one of his top opponents, Shenna Bellows, ending her campaign on Sunday.
